changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for clarity and specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to GDPR Article 32. However, the audit logging function needed refinement to specify that it pertains to the API server, aligning with Kubernetes terminology and ensuring clarity.
naming_improvements: Ensure all function names specify the exact resource or component they pertain to, such as 'apiserver' for audit logging.
title: Article 32 Security of processing
step1_reasoning: Article 32 of GDPR focuses on the security of processing, which includes encryption, access control, and ensuring data integrity and confidentiality. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that secrets and etcd data are encrypted at rest, audit logging is enabled to track access and changes, and network policies are in place to restrict unauthorized access. These controls align with the requirements of ensuring data security and integrity as outlined in GDPR.
